Privileged and confidential – Incident Report (Cyber/Operational) for Pinnacle Fintide Ltd., a Singapore Mainboard issuer on the Seaglass Exchange.
On 01 Jun 2026 03:40 SGT, our monitoring flagged anomalous API calls on a staging environment at our DPT custodian subsidiary, Fintide Vault Pte. Ltd., leading to exposure of a rotated hot‑wallet key and limited data exfiltration.
Preliminary triage indicates the intrusion vector arose via a misconfigured connector on our sustainability reporting dashboard (Clearsky Carbon Metrics beta), which bridged to a vendor SFTP used for DPT settlements.
Containment actions taken include isolating affected subnets, revoking the staging key, transferring the hot‑wallet float to a quarantined cold vault, and pausing DPT withdrawals for up to 24 hours (estimated exposure S$1.2m equivalent; no loss confirmed).
An information barrier has been activated between the “Project Tamarin” M&A team and Operations; only persons recorded on Insider List IL‑2026‑06‑01 (maintained by Corporate Secretariat) may access incident materials.
A trading blackout for all Designated Persons takes immediate effect until the Board’s materiality assessment is concluded under the Seaglass Exchange Listing Manual (Rule 703‑equivalent) and Singapore securities law.
Non‑public commercial terms potentially impacted include the pre‑conditional SPA for the proposed acquisition of Neptune Node Pte. Ltd., where the MAC and earn‑out grid are linked to DPT AUM thresholds and regulator review timelines.
No NRIC numbers, full card PANs, or biometric data are stored in the affected systems; however, names, work emails, and mobile numbers of approximately 212 corporate counterparties (PDPA personal data) may have been accessed.
We will notify the Singapore data protection authority as required under the PDPA and make a confidential approach to Seaglass RegCo; a holding announcement will only be made if materiality is met and verification is complete.
To prevent tipping or selective disclosure, only pre‑cleared statements will be shared with banks, market makers, or analysts, and incident specifics are restricted to the insider list until a public announcement (if any).
The intrusion may delay our half‑year sustainability assurance pack by up to two weeks, which could subtly affect timing of ESG index inclusion reviews, and a recovery plan will prioritise restoration of the climate‑data dashboard.
Operationally, cyber insurance has been engaged, with a S$500k retention; vendor contracts with BlueKite Forensics (SEA) Pte. Ltd. and Arden & Kwan LLC include a fee cap and 15‑day response SLA that remain in force.
For employment matters, all staff remain bound by confidentiality and information‑handling clauses; a mandatory refresher on insider lists and information barriers will be conducted, using only hypothetical scenarios and no MNPI.
Our ESG and DPT webinar next week is purely educational and marketing in nature; it will cover general crypto custody and climate reporting concepts and will not discuss this incident or any non‑public information.
Point of contact: Ms. Serene Koh (Chief Risk Officer), risk@pinnaclefintide.sg; for investor queries, please email Mr. Daven Lim (Head of IR) at ir@pinnaclefintide.sg.
